Q15.v: <<UNVERIFIED: programs.md does not state total scheme beneficiary count; CAG audit and OUTCOME section confirm AI procedures tracked annually but provide no aggregate beneficiary figure>>
Q15.src: programs.md — OUTCOME: 'AI procedures completed annually' (no aggregate HH count); CAG State Accounts 2024-25

Q16.v: <<UNVERIFIED: absolute number unavailable; however, by scheme design all beneficiaries are local farming households with bovine livestock in their home area — technicians travel to farms, not farmers to a central location; θ₂ ≈ 1.0 by design>>
Q16.src: programs.md — ANNOUNCEMENT: 'AI service provider network' (field delivery model implies local beneficiaries); PORTAL: 'service availability' at local level

Q17.v: Settled_communities — within GP/block of the farmer's home village where AI service is delivered
Q17.r: AI services are delivered by itinerant technicians to farmers at their home farm/village. Beneficiaries are settled farming households within the operational block/district. The 10 km radius definition applies; effectively all beneficiaries are within the scheme's operational commons area since the service is delivered to their doorstep.
Q17.src: programs.md — ANNOUNCEMENT: 'AI service provider network' (field delivery); PORTAL: 'service availability, technician network'

Q18.v: <<UNVERIFIED: absolute figures for Q15 and Q16 unavailable; θ₂ estimated ≈ 1.00 by scheme design (all registered beneficiaries are local farming households served at their home village by field technicians; no non-local beneficiaries exist in scheme design)>>
Q18.calc: θ₂ = Q16.v ÷ Q15.v — not computable from available data; design-based estimate θ₂ ≈ 1.00
Q18.interp: UNVERIFIED — design strongly suggests PASS (θ₂ ≈ 1.00); re-verify with MIS beneficiary register

Q19.v: <<UNVERIFIED: programs.md does not provide beneficiary categorisation data by SC/ST/marginalised status; SECC or PM-JANMAN data not referenced in scheme documents>>
Q19.r: Chhattisgarh's rural population is approximately 43% SC/ST (ST ~30%, SC ~13% per Census 2011). AI program targets all livestock-owning rural farming households. In Chhattisgarh, ST and SC communities have significant livestock ownership. Including women-headed, landless, and elderly-headed households, θ₃ ≥ 0.40 is demographically plausible but not confirmed from scheme MIS data.
Q19.src: programs.md — no beneficiary categorisation data; Census of India 2011 (Chhattisgarh SC/ST population reference — external verified source)

Q20.v: <<UNVERIFIED: θ₃ not computable without Q19 figure; demographic evidence supports likely PASS>>
Q20.calc: θ₃ = Q19.v ÷ Q15.v — not computable; demographic inference suggests θ₃ likely ≥ 0.40 given Chhattisgarh ST/SC rural population composition
Q20.interp: UNVERIFIED — demographic profile of Chhattisgarh rural farmers supports likely PASS; must be verified with scheme MIS beneficiary data categorised by social group
Q20.src: programs.md — no categorised beneficiary data; Census of India 2011 SC/ST data for Chhattisgarh

22a.r: No formally constituted community representative body (Gram Sabha, User Group, JFM Committee, Village Water Committee, SHG federation, BMC, dairy cooperative with decision-making mandate, or equivalent) is mentioned in programs.md. The ANNOUNCEMENT describes an 'AI service provider network' which is a technical delivery mechanism, not a community governance body. No community institution with scheme decision-making powers is referenced in any section.

22b.r: No community body role is mandated in scheme guidelines as documented in programs.md. The scheme operates as an administrative service delivery mechanism (government technicians → individual farmers) without a community governance layer.

22c.r: No meeting records, minutes, or resolutions for any community body can be verified. No community body exists per 22a, so functionality cannot be assessed.

Q22.src: programs.md — ANNOUNCEMENT: 'AI service provider network, farmer eligibility and registration, SOP, cost-sharing mechanism' (no community body mentioned); BUDGET, SURVEY, OUTCOME, CAG, PORTAL — none reference a community governance institution

Q23.r: No governance body exists per Q22a; women's representation in any such body cannot be assessed. T2.5 = 0 by extension. Enhancement gap noted. Programs.md does not reference women-specific governance provisions.
Q23.src: programs.md — no governance body or women's representation data referenced

Q24.v: <<UNVERIFIED: no scheme-specific grievance mechanism documented in programs.md; National Portal listing suggests general government grievance channels (PM-JANMAN portal, CM Helpline 1100) are accessible but scheme-level mandated grievance mechanism not confirmed>>
Q24.r: No formal grievance mechanism is documented at scheme level in programs.md. The scheme is on the National Portal which provides a general interface, but a scheme-specific, institutionally mandated grievance mechanism for non-beneficiaries has not been verified. Governance gap flagged.
Q24.src: programs.md — PORTAL section (National Portal listing only); CM Helpline (1100) exists at state level (general governance, not scheme-specific)
